query: give me more details on Trump admin export controls
answer: The Trump administration's AI export control policy has been one of the most consequential — and chaotic — regulatory stories of 2026. The administration first scrapped the Biden-era AI Diffusion Rule in May 2025, which had created a tiered licensing framework for chip exports to different countries. That cancellation inadvertently created a loophole: Chinese subsidiaries operating outside China could buy advanced Nvidia chips without a license. On May 31, 2026, the Commerce Department issued emergency guidance to close that gap, clarifying that a license is required to sell chips to any firm whose parent company is headquartered in a restricted country, regardless of where the subsidiary is located. The FDD has noted this enforcement gap may have allowed an untold number of high-end chips to reach China's military-industrial base — including entities developing nuclear weapons and offensive cyberattacks — over the past year. On the AI model side, the administration took unprecedented action on June 12, 2026, ordering Anthropic to immediately suspend global access to its two most advanced models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all foreign nationals — including citizens of G7 allies — citing national security. This is the first known instance of the U.S. government forcing a frontier AI company to take its top models offline, and it has emerged as a key risk factor in Anthropic's confidential IPO filing. Anthropic is currently in daily White House negotiations to develop a joint framework for assessing AI security risks. Meanwhile, Congress is advancing legislation that would require GPS trackers to be embedded in every Nvidia chip exported from the U.S., after paperwork-based controls repeatedly failed to prevent restricted chips from reaching China.
